data.output:
# DDx Story 12 — Optimize the prompts used to operate `ddx run` (final)

## Scope clarification

`ddx run` is a thin pass-through (`service_run.go:114`, `run_internal.go`) —
no DDx-owned system prompt. The static prompts DDx ships on every attempt and
that the user actually means to optimize are:

1. `executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText` — execute_bead.go:1208 — ~1078 words / ~1400 tok
2. `executeBeadInstructionsAgentText`  — execute_bead.go:1274 — ~1020 words / ~1330 tok
3. `beadReviewInstructions`            — execute_bead_review.go:124 — ~283 words / ~370 tok (LEAVE)
4. `executeBeadMissingGoverningText`   — execute_bead.go:1357 — ~42 words (LEAVE)

Selector at execute_bead.go:1343 routes `agent`/`fiz`/`embedded` to the Agent
variant; everything else (`claude`, `codex`, `opencode`, unknown) uses the
Claude variant. This grouping is intentional — see plan-2026-04-18 §112 and
the portable-prompt principle (frame/principles.md:152). Story 12 keeps the
two-variant split.

## Diagnosis

### Load-bearing guardrails (every one MUST survive any rewrite)

Codex audit caught the original list missing several. Final list:

- AC checkbox is satisfied by a specific code/test/file (anti-handwave) — execute_bead.go:1208,1234
- Read first / read named files / read referenced specs before editing — execute_bead.go:1232,1300
- Missing-governing fallback note — execute_bead.go:1357
- Commit subject ends with `[<bead-id>]` — execute_bead.go:1238,1308
- Commit exactly once, when green — execute_bead.go:1238,1308
- `git add <specific-paths>`, never `git add -A` — execute_bead.go:1238,1308
- Do not commit red code — execute_bead.go:1236,1306
- Do not modify files outside bead scope — execute_bead.go:1261
- Never run `ddx init` — execute_bead.go:1260,1334
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — execute_bead.go:1259,1333
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md unless asked — execute_bead.go:1262,1335
- Bead description overrides CLAUDE.md / YAGNI defaults — execute_bead.go:1248,1328
- Reports go under `{{.AttemptDir}}/`, never `/tmp`, and are committed alongside code — execute_bead.go:1244,1318
- Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` before exiting empty — execute_bead.go:1240,1314
- Step 0 size-check + decomposition path — execute_bead.go:1210,1278
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do NOT declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open — execute_bead.go:1254,1324 (Agent variant has stronger phrasing)
- Stop after the commit succeeds (Agent post-commit runaway guard) — execute_bead.go:1310
- Agent-only: use tool calls, not `bash: cat`/`rg`/`ls` — execute_bead.go:1304
- Reviewer JSON fence + anti-echo rules — execute_bead_review.go:132–151

### Redundancy across the two execute-bead variants

Step 0 size check, "investigation reports under {{.AttemptDir}}/", "review
gate / `<review-findings>`", "bead overrides project defaults", and most of
the constraints tail are duplicated almost verbatim. Source-level dedup will
remove ~250 words of repeated text. Per-attempt dedup (after tightening)
targets ~30%+ word reduction in the rendered prompt.

### Note on prompt caching (codex catch)

`buildPrompt` at execute_bead.go:1392–1461 places dynamic bead metadata FIRST
and static instructions LAST. That means Anthropic's prefix-cache cannot help
at all today — every attempt cache-misses on the static suffix. Reordering
to put static instructions first would unlock cache hits across attempts
with the same prompt version. **Capture this as a follow-up bead** (B5) but
do not block Story 12 on it; reordering changes XML semantics and merits
its own change with separate review.

## Spec changes

### FEAT-022 amendment

Add under Functional / Shared evidence primitives:

> **Static-prompt minimum-prompt rule.** Every static instruction string DDx
> embeds in an LLM prompt MUST declare its load-bearing guardrails inline
> as a Go comment list above the constant, one bullet per guardrail with a
> pointer to the failure mode it prevents. Edits MUST preserve every listed
> guardrail. New guardrails MUST be added to the list. A regression test
> MUST assert each listed guardrail still appears in the rendered prompt.
> This rule applies to STATIC prompt bodies; dynamic evidence egress is
> covered by the §5–§7 cap primitives and is unaffected.

The closing sentence is per codex's correction: do not let this amendment
imply static-comment guardrails replace the existing dynamic-egress caps.

### New TD: prompt versioning + offline comparison + rendered-size report

Captures the measurement roadmap. Summary:

- Add `prompt_sha` (and later `prompt_version`) to `manifest.json` so
  attempt outcomes slice cleanly across rewrites using existing telemetry
  (usage.json, session_index.go session tokens, resolver_feat008.go
  efficacy view).
- Add a deterministic test fixture that renders both variants for a
  representative bead and reports byte / word counts, gated as a
  CI artifact (catches accidental bloat).
- Optional: `DDX_PROMPT_VARIANT=<name>` selector for offline A/B.

## Implementation plan

1. **B1 — Extract shared blocks (refactor only).**
   - Pull duplicated text into named constants:
     `instrStep0SizeCheck`, `instrInvestigationReports`,
     `instrReviewGate`, `instrBeadOverride`, `instrCoreConstraints`.
   - Both variants compose: per-harness preamble + per-harness process body
     + shared blocks.
   - Rendered prompt MUST be byte-identical (or trivially equivalent —
     whitespace only) to the current output. All existing tests pass
     unchanged.

2. **B2 — Tighten shared blocks for density.**
   - Step 0: prose → 4-bullet checklist + 4-step recipe (~120 → ~80 w).
   - Process: drop verification-command examples (the bead names them);
     keep "do not commit red code" as one bullet; keep `[<bead-id>]`,
     `git add <specific-paths>`, "commit exactly once", "stop after
     commit", "do not modify outside bead scope" as separate bullets
     (codex catch — must remain explicit). (~150 → ~85 w)
   - Review-gate: two bullets ("review is a gate"; "address every BLOCKING
     `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking
     findings open"). (~80 → ~45 w)
   - Bead-override: one sentence. (~70 → ~25 w)
   - Constraints tail: bullets, including `.ddx/executions/` intact and
     "never `ddx init`" as separate items. (~60 → ~40 w)
   - Add the load-bearing-guardrail comment block above each constant per
     FEAT-022 amendment.

3. **B3 — Render-size report + `prompt_sha` in manifest (lightweight v1
   measurement).**
   - Test fixture renders representative-bead prompt for each
     (variant, contextBudget) and prints word / byte count to a CI artifact.
   - `manifest.json` gains `prompt_sha` (sha256 of the rendered prompt).
     Existing analytics in resolver_feat008.go can group attempts by
     prompt_sha for before/after comparison. Codex insisted on shipping
     measurement in v1, not deferring entirely.

4. **B4 — Amend FEAT-022 + author TD.**
   - FEAT-022 minimum-prompt rule paragraph.
   - New TD captures `prompt_version`, `DDX_PROMPT_VARIANT` selector, and
     the offline-comparison roadmap (NOT implemented in this story).

5. **B5 — (deferred / optional) Reorder buildPrompt to put static
   instructions first for prefix-cache compatibility.** Not on Story 12
   critical path; file the bead, do not work it here.

## Acceptance criteria

- AC1: After B1, both variants share ≥4 named instruction-block constants;
  no shared block text is duplicated across variants in source.
- AC2: After B2, rendered prompt for the representative bead is ≥30% shorter
  (word count) than current for both Claude and Agent variants.
- AC3: New test `TestExecuteBeadInstructionsLoadBearingGuardrails` asserts
  every guardrail listed in the file-level comment block (final list above)
  appears in the rendered prompt for each (harness, variant). Includes
  selector tests: `agent`/`fiz`/`embedded` → Agent; `claude`/`codex`/
  `opencode`/unknown → Claude (per codex catch).
- AC4: New invariant tests assert: rendered prompts contain
  `[<bead-id>]`, `git add <specific-paths>`, no `git add -A`,
  `no_changes_rationale.txt`, `.ddx/executions/`, `ddx bead create`,
  `ddx bead dep add`, `ddx bead update` (per codex catch).
- AC5: New test asserts non-minimal prompts include
  `executeBeadMissingGoverningText`; `contextBudget == "minimal"` omits
  governing refs as today.
- AC6: All existing prompt tests pass unchanged
  (`execute_bead_evidence_path_test.go`, `review_verdict_test.go`,
  `execute_bead_failure_mode_test.go`).
- AC7: `manifest.json` contains `prompt_sha` for every attempt; size-report
  fixture emits word/byte counts per (variant, contextBudget).
- AC8: FEAT-022 amended; new TD authored.
- AC9: `beadReviewInstructions` is unchanged. (NOT "out of scope" — the
  schema/fence guardrails are load-bearing and dynamic-evidence cost is
  already addressed by `BuildReviewPromptBounded`. Per codex.)
- AC10: No new per-harness branches beyond Claude/Agent.

## Tests (consolidated)

- Existing: `go test ./cli/internal/agent/...` passes unchanged.
- New: AC3 guardrail-list test.
- New: AC3 selector test.
- New: AC4 substring-invariant tests.
- New: AC5 missing-governing test.
- New: AC2 size-floor test (word count threshold per variant).
- New: AC9 reviewer-prompt regression test (fenced-JSON requirement, no
  prose, no template echo) — explicit re-assertion.

## Risks (post-codex)

- Guardrail loss — mitigated by FEAT-022 comment-list contract + AC3 test.
- Story 10 collision on Step 0 — serialize. (See coordination.)
- Per-harness drift — shared blocks; per-variant only owns preamble +
  process body.
- Cheap-tier behavior change on shorter prompts — `prompt_sha` in manifest
  + size-report fixture (B3) lets us slice efficacy/no_evidence_produced
  across the rewrite. If the metric worsens, revert is one constant edit.
- FEAT-022 amendment phrasing must NOT imply static-comment guardrails
  replace dynamic-egress caps (codex catch).
- `beadReviewInstructions` left alone is correct; reviewer's real cost is
  diff inlining, already bounded by `BuildReviewPromptBounded`.
- Prompt caching unaddressed in Story 12; B5 captured separately.

## Bead breakdown (final, with codex's split on B4)

- **B1 — Extract shared instruction blocks (refactor, byte-equivalent
  output).** Tier: cheap. Deps: Story 10 prompt edits merged.
  AC: AC1, AC6.
- **B2 — Tighten shared blocks; add guardrail-list comment per FEAT-022;
  add invariant + selector + missing-governing tests.** Tier: cheap;
  review at smart. Deps: B1.
  AC: AC2, AC3, AC4, AC5, AC6, AC10.
- **B3 — Add `prompt_sha` to manifest.json + render-size CI fixture.**
  Tier: cheap. Deps: none (can run in parallel with B1/B2 — treats prompt
  as opaque).
  AC: AC7.
- **B4a — FEAT-022 amendment (minimum-prompt rule).** Tier: cheap. Deps:
  B2 (so the rule reflects what was done).
  AC: AC8 (FEAT-022 portion).
- **B4b — TD for prompt versioning, `DDX_PROMPT_VARIANT` selector,
  offline comparison.** Tier: cheap. Deps: B4a.
  AC: AC8 (TD portion).
- **B5 (optional, not part of Story 12 critical path) — Reorder
  `buildPrompt` to place static instructions before bead metadata for
  prefix-cache compatibility.** Tier: standard. Deps: B2. File now,
  prioritize separately.

## Coordination with Story 10

Story 10 (automatic escalation + always-review) has not yet returned a plan
file (`/tmp/story-10-final.md` does not exist as of this write). Story 10
edits the SAME Step 0 / decomposition section that B1+B2 dedup and tighten.
**Hard recommendation:** do not start B1 or B2 until Story 10's prompt
edits land; if both stories drain in the same run, file B1 with a hard
`dep` on Story 10's terminal bead. **Parallelizable carve-outs (codex):**
B3 (prompt_sha + size fixture) and B4b (TD authoring, if it avoids
quoting Step 0 final wording) can run in parallel with Story 10. B4a
should wait until B2 is done.

7	# Technical Design: Config-Driven Runtime Options
8	
9	## Type Definitions
10	
11	### `config.ResolvedConfig`
12	
13	Immutable value type. All fields unexported. Consumers call methods.
14	
15	Construction is sealed: zero-value `ResolvedConfig{}` and
16	`var rcfg ResolvedConfig` are valid Go expressions, but every public
17	accessor checks a sentinel field on first read and panics with a
18	specific message naming `LoadAndResolve` if the sentinel is unset.
19	This makes a zero-value `ResolvedConfig` syntactically constructible
20	but operationally unusable.
21	
22	```go
23	package config
24	
25	// ResolvedConfig is the loop/runner/reviewer's view of merged
26	// project config plus per-invocation overrides. It is constructed
27	// only by (*Config).Resolve and is safe to share across goroutines.
28	//
29	// Sealed: every accessor calls (r ResolvedConfig).requireSealed()
30	// on entry. A zero-value ResolvedConfig fails loudly on first read.
31	type ResolvedConfig struct {
32	    // sealed is set to true by Resolve. Accessors panic if false.
33	    sealed bool
34	
35	    // unexported fields holding the merged values
36	    assignee                 string
37	    reviewMaxRetries         int
38	    noProgressCooldown       time.Duration
39	    maxNoChangesBeforeClose  int
40	    heartbeatInterval        time.Duration

177:	// PromptSHA is the sha256 (hex) of the rendered prompt bytes written to
179:	// analytics can group attempts by prompt_sha to compare outcomes across
181:	PromptSHA string `json:"prompt_sha,omitempty"`
1127:		PromptSHA: promptSHA(promptContent),
1478:// Used as manifest.prompt_sha so analytics can group attempts by the exact

---
ddx:
  id: TD-028
  depends_on:
    - FEAT-022
  status: draft
  implementation: deferred
---
# Technical Design: Prompt Versioning, `DDX_PROMPT_VARIANT` Selector, and Offline Comparison

## Status

Design only. **Implementation is deferred.** This TD captures the
measurement roadmap that grew out of Story 12 (optimize the static
prompts that drive `ddx agent execute-bead`). Story 12 lands the
mechanical pieces — guardrail dedup (B1/B2) and `prompt_sha` on the
attempt manifest (B3). Everything below — the human-readable
`prompt_version` field, the `DDX_PROMPT_VARIANT` environment selector
for offline A/B, and the offline-comparison roadmap that consumes
them — is intentionally NOT implemented in Story 12. A follow-up
story will pick this up once we have enough post-Story-12 attempts
in `.ddx/executions/` to know which slices actually move the cheap-
tier metrics.

## Motivation

The Story 12 rewrite tightens two ~1,000-word static prompts
(`executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText`, `executeBeadInstructionsAgentText`
in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go`) by ~30% and adds a
load-bearing-guardrail comment contract (FEAT-022 amendment). Two
risks fall out of that work:

1. **No way to tell whether the rewrite helped.** Cheap-tier behavior
   on the new shorter prompts could be better, the same, or worse, and
   we have no clean way to slice attempt outcomes (efficacy,
   `no_evidence_produced`, escalation rate) by prompt body.
2. **No way to A/B alternative wordings without shipping them.** Once
   the rewrite lands, every future tightening is a flying-blind edit
   to a constant, with the same measurement gap.

B3 closes part of (1) by writing `prompt_sha` (sha256 hex of the
rendered prompt bytes) into every attempt's `manifest.json`. That is
sufficient for a coarse before/after slice, but it has two
limitations:

- A sha is opaque. A reviewer reading an analytics dashboard cannot
  tell from `prompt_sha=ab12…` what version of the prompt produced
  the row.
- It only differentiates whatever variant DDx happened to ship at the
  time. There is no mechanism to run an experimental variant
  side-by-side with the live one.

This TD describes how we close both gaps without growing the
runtime surface area.

## Design

### 1. `prompt_version` field on the attempt manifest

`AttemptManifest` already carries `prompt_sha` (see
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` field `PromptSHA` at line 177
and `promptSHA(...)` at line 1478). Add an adjacent string field
`prompt_version` populated from a per-(harness, variant) constant
defined alongside the prompt body:

```go
const (
    // executeBeadPromptVersionClaude is bumped by hand whenever
    // executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText changes in a way that
    // analytics should treat as a new prompt. Format:
    // "<story>-<harness>-<n>", e.g. "story12-claude-1".
    executeBeadPromptVersionClaude = "story12-claude-1"
    executeBeadPromptVersionAgent  = "story12-agent-1"
)
```

Rules:

- The version string is a developer-curated label, NOT computed.
  Whitespace-only edits do not bump it; semantic edits do. The
  guardrail-list comment block above each prompt constant
  (FEAT-022 amendment) is the natural place to record the version
  bump alongside the change.
- `prompt_sha` remains the source of truth for "is this attempt
  byte-identical to that one." `prompt_version` is the human label
  used for grouping in dashboards and changelogs.
- Both fields are written into `manifest.json`. Existing readers
  that ignore unknown fields are unaffected.

Why both: a sha alone forces every analytics consumer to maintain
its own sha→label mapping. A label alone allows accidental
collisions (developer forgets to bump). Carrying both keeps the
sha as the authoritative grouping key and the label as the
display name.

### 2. `DDX_PROMPT_VARIANT` environment selector

Add an opt-in environment variable read at prompt-render time:

- `DDX_PROMPT_VARIANT` unset (default): render the live prompt as
  today. Behavior is identical to current code paths.
- `DDX_PROMPT_VARIANT=<name>`: select an experimental prompt body
  registered in a small in-process registry, e.g.
  `map[string]promptBundle` keyed by `<name>`, where
  `promptBundle` contains the `{claudeText, agentText, version}`
  triple.

Constraints:

- The selector ONLY swaps the static body. The harness selector
  (`agent`/`fiz`/`embedded` → Agent variant, everything else →
  Claude variant; see `execute_bead.go:1343`) is unchanged. An
  experimental bundle must supply BOTH variants.
- Unknown variant name is a hard error at render time, not a
  silent fallback. Operators running an A/B want loud failure if
  they typo the variant name.
- The variant name (and its version label) is recorded on the
  manifest as `prompt_variant` and folded into `prompt_version`
  (e.g. `experiment-tighter-step0-claude-1`). `prompt_sha` still
  hashes the actual rendered bytes, so it remains the
  authoritative grouping key.
- Variants live in source under
  `cli/internal/agent/prompt_variants/` with the same load-bearing-
  guardrail comment contract as the live prompts. A variant that
  drops a guardrail fails the FEAT-022 regression test, same as an
  edit to the live prompt would.
- Variants are NOT a config-file concept. They are compiled in.
  This keeps offline experimentation a developer activity, not an
  end-user surface, and avoids inventing a new config schema for
  something we expect to retire after each round of tuning.

Why an env var and not a CLI flag: `ddx agent execute-bead` is
invoked by the loop (`agent execute-loop` / `ddx work`), and the
loop does not currently thread per-call CLI flags into the
prompt-render path. An env var is the cheapest way to attach a
variant selection to a whole drain run without rewriting the
loop's argv plumbing.

### 3. Offline-comparison roadmap

The combination of `prompt_sha`, `prompt_version`, and
`prompt_variant` on the manifest lets us reuse the telemetry that
already exists in the repo, without standing up new infrastructure:

- **`.ddx/executions/<run-id>/manifest.json`** — the per-attempt
  record this TD extends. Already includes outcome, harness, bead
  id, base rev, and (post-B3) `prompt_sha`. Adding
  `prompt_version` and `prompt_variant` here is the only schema
  change this roadmap requires.
- **`.ddx/executions/<run-id>/usage.json`** — token usage emitted
  by harnesses that report it. Joined to the manifest by
  `<run-id>`, this gives a per-prompt-version cost profile.
- **`session_index.go`** (`cli/internal/agent/session_index.go`)
  — already maintains the session-token index that ties an
  attempt's run id to its harness session events. No change
  required; new fields ride along.
- **`resolver_feat008.go`** (`cli/internal/server/graphql/`) —
  exposes the efficacy view (`EfficacyRows`, `EfficacyAttempts`).
  The efficacy resolver already aggregates per attempt; extending
  it to group/filter by `prompt_version` or `prompt_variant` is a
  resolver-only change once the manifest fields are populated.

Concretely, the offline workflow becomes:

1. Operator sets `DDX_PROMPT_VARIANT=experiment-foo` and runs
   `ddx work` over a representative bead queue (or replays a
   captured queue with `--from <rev>`).
2. Each attempt writes a manifest tagged
   `prompt_variant=experiment-foo`,
   `prompt_version=experiment-foo-claude-1`, plus the rendered
   `prompt_sha`.
3. The efficacy view (extended) groups attempts by
   `prompt_version` and reports outcome distribution, retry
   count, escalation rate, mean tokens, and mean wall time per
   group. A second drain with the variant unset or set to a
   different name produces the comparison set.
4. If the experiment wins, promote the variant: copy its body
   over the live constant, bump the live version label, and
   delete the variant entry. If it loses, delete the variant.

Out of scope for this TD:

- Statistical significance testing. The cheap-tier sample sizes
  in our queue are too small for clean significance claims;
  decisions will be directional ("variant clearly worse → drop"
  or "variant clearly better → ship"), not p-value gated.
- Online A/B (random-assigning live attempts to variants). The
  selector is offline-only by design — a single run uses a single
  variant. Online A/B would require per-attempt variant
  assignment, manifest schema changes beyond this TD, and a story
  of its own.
- Cross-project comparison. The efficacy view is per-project; a
  variant evaluated in one project's queue is not automatically
  comparable to another's.

### Interactions with existing code

- `buildPrompt` (`execute_bead.go:1392`): the natural insertion
  point for variant lookup. It already owns prompt assembly; it
  would gain a `selectVariant(harness, os.Getenv(...))` call
  before reading the static constants.
- `manifest.json` writer
  (`AttemptManifest` struct + the `PromptSHA:` write at
  `execute_bead.go:1127`): gains two adjacent string fields. Same
  serialization path.
- `executions_mirror.go`: already mirrors manifests into
  `.ddx/executions/` for the resolver to read. No change.
- `BuildReviewPromptBounded` and `beadReviewInstructions`: not
  in scope. The reviewer prompt is intentionally untouched by
  Story 12 (AC9) and remains untouched by this TD.

## Non-goals

- This TD does not propose a new config-file surface. No
  `.ddx.yml` keys, no new commands.
- This TD does not propose runtime variant rotation. One drain
  uses one variant.
- This TD does not propose changing the harness selector. The
  Claude/Agent split (`execute_bead.go:1343`) is preserved per
  Story 12 AC10.
- This TD does not propose statistical-test infrastructure.

## Migration / rollout

When a follow-up story implements this:

1. Land `prompt_version` first, defaulting to the current Story
   12 labels. Manifests gain the field; nothing else changes.
   Backfill is unnecessary — historical manifests stay on
   `prompt_sha` only, and the resolver treats missing
   `prompt_version` as a sentinel ("pre-versioning").
2. Land the `DDX_PROMPT_VARIANT` selector and the variants
   directory. Default behavior (variable unset) is unchanged.
3. Extend the efficacy resolver's grouping last. Until then,
   offline comparison is "grep manifests by `prompt_version`."

## Open questions

- Should `prompt_version` be enforced via a unit test that fails
  when the constant body changes without a corresponding version
  bump? A naive sha-of-body equality check is workable but
  noisy on whitespace edits. Defer the decision to the
  implementing story; the FEAT-022 guardrail-list comment
  contract already gives reviewers a place to catch missed
  bumps.
- Should the variant registry live in a `prompt_variants_test.go`
  build-tagged file rather than production code, so experimental
  bodies do not ship in the binary? Probably yes for variants
  intended only for one-off offline runs; defer.

## References

- Story 12 plan: `/tmp/story-12-final.md` (working doc, see
  "New TD" section).
- FEAT-022: `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-022-prompt-evidence-assembly.md`
  (minimum-prompt rule amendment from B4a).
- Live prompt constants:
  `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go`
  (`executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText` ~line 1208,
  `executeBeadInstructionsAgentText` ~line 1274,
  harness selector ~line 1343, `buildPrompt` ~line 1392).
- Manifest field landed in B3:
  `AttemptManifest.PromptSHA` (~line 177),
  populated at the manifest write site (~line 1127),
  computed by `promptSHA` (~line 1478).
- Telemetry consumed by the offline-comparison roadmap:
  `cli/internal/agent/session_index.go`,
  `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go`
  (efficacy view), per-run `usage.json` written by harnesses.
